heading: Biomass-based diesel and biodiesel labeling
label: 17021 Biomass-based diesel and biodiesel labeling
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Each retail diesel fuel pump shall be labeled in a manner that informs consumers of the percent of biomass-based diesel or biodiesel that is contained in the biomass-based diesel blend or biodiesel blend that is offered for sale, as determined by the Federal Trade Commission.

Biomass-based diesel blends or biodiesel blends that contain less than or equal to 5 percent biomass-based diesel or biodiesel by volume and that meet ASTM D975 diesel specifications shall not require any additional labels.

Biomass-based diesel blends or biodiesel blends that contain more than 5 percent biomass-based diesel or biodiesel by volume but not more than 20 percent by volume shall be labeled “contains biomass-based diesel or biodiesel in quantities between 5 percent and 20 percent”.
Biomass-based diesel or biodiesel blends that contain more than 20 percent biomass based or biodiesel by volume shall be labeled “contains more than 20 percent biomass-based diesel or biodiesel”.

children.heading: ASTM
children.label: 1 ASTM
children.text: The term “ASTM” means the American Society of Testing and Materials.

children.heading: Biomass-based diesel
children.label: 2 Biomass-based diesel
children.text: The term “biomass-based diesel” means biodiesel as defined in .section 13220(f) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the registration requirements for fuels and fuel additives under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the requirements of ASTM standard D6751.

children.heading: Biomass-based diesel and biodiesel blends
children.label: 4 Biomass-based diesel and biodiesel blends
children.text: The terms “biomass-based diesel blend” and “biodiesel blend” means a blend of “biomass-based diesel” or “biodiesel” fuel that is blended with petroleum-based diesel fuel.
